codeReview.summary: The activity-logging feature is comprehensively implemented with excellent code quality. All required log actions (created, claimed, released, updated, deleted) are properly implemented with structured details. The GET /api/logs endpoint supports all required filters (task_id, agent_id, project_id, action) and handles combined filtering correctly. The implementation follows project conventions and integrates seamlessly with existing task operations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the established pattern of separating business logic into src/services/ (taskLogs.ts) and route handlers into src/api/routes/ (logs.ts). This aligns with AGENTS.md conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/taskLogs.ts contains pure business logic for logging; src/api/routes/logs.ts handles HTTP concerns

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly used Zod for validation in the logs endpoint, following the established pattern from other routes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/api/routes/logs.ts:12-19 defines logsQuerySchema with UUID regex validation and enum for actions

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly handles the audit trail requirement by NOT making task_logs.task_id a foreign key. This allows logs to persist after task deletion, which is explicitly documented in the schema comment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/db/schema.ts:62-69 - taskId is text (not foreign key) with explanatory comment about audit trails

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly implemented the project_id filter using a JOIN with the tasks table since task_logs doesn't have a direct project_id column. This shows good understanding of the data model.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/taskLogs.ts:145-175 - getTaskLogs function handles projectId filter with innerJoin to tasks table

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test coverage is comprehensive with 26 tests covering all validation contract assertions (VAL-LOG-001 through VAL-LOG-007). Tests include happy path, validation errors, and edge cases like log persistence after task deletion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/api/logs.test.ts - 26 test cases with detailed verification of each action type and filter combination

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker created a reusable resetDatabase helper in tests/db/reset.ts to ensure clean test state. This follows testing best practices and is used across multiple test files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/db/reset.ts - exports resetDatabase function that deletes data in correct order respecting FK constraints

summary: The activity-logging feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The implementation includes: (1) Task logging service with 5 action types (created, claimed, released, updated, deleted) each with structured details, (2) GET /api/logs endpoint with full filter support, (3) Integration into all task operations, (4) Schema changes to support audit trail (logs persist after task deletion), (5) Comprehensive test suite with 26 tests covering all validation contract assertions. Code quality is high with proper error handling, TypeScript types, and adherence to project conventions. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
